transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Morning team. This is our Faith and Family feature stress test for Lever. I'll be honest — we don't have domain-specific data pulled for this module yet, so today's job is to identify what's missing, what needs building, and what content opportunities exist. Let's be direct about gaps. Elon, what's the current state of Faith and Family in the app?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: So right now, Faith and Family is essentially a placeholder. We have the category shell but there's no dedicated tracking, no journaling prompts, no family goal-setting workflows — nothing that makes it feel like a real lever. If we're serious about this being a core pillar alongside health and wealth, we need at minimum: a daily reflection or devotional prompt system, a family relationship tracker with configurable touchpoints, and some kind of accountability metric that ties into the overall Lever score. The architecture can support all of this, but nobody's specced it out yet. That's the bottleneck.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: From a revenue angle, Faith and Family is actually the sleeper differentiator. Every productivity app does habits and finances — almost none of them touch this space meaningfully. If we nail it, that's our moat with the faith-based market, which is massive and underserved in tech. But we can't monetize what doesn't exist yet, so building this out is a revenue conversation too. Nothing actionable on my approved items specifically ties to Faith and Family, but I'd flag that we need Joshua's eyes on this as a strategic priority.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content-wise, this is wide open and honestly exciting. Faith and Family content — daily devotionals, marriage check-in prompts, parenting frameworks, legacy planning — that stuff generates insane engagement in the faith-based demo. I can start with a blog post that positions Lever's Faith and Family vision, teases what's coming, and drives waitlist signups. [AGENT] Draft next blog post — I'll angle it around the Faith and Family pillar specifically and why most apps ignore the most important levers in life.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Let's do a quick round of cross-talk. Elon, what does Alex need from you to make that blog post technically accurate about what's coming?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: I can spec out a one-pager on the Faith and Family feature roadmap — devotional engine, family touchpoint tracker, and the scoring integration — so Alex isn't writing fiction. Give me 48 hours. But I need Joshua to actually sign off on what devotional content looks like, because that's a theological and personal call, not an engineering one.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Agreed with Elon on that. Joshua needs to own the content voice for anything faith-related. That's brand-defining territory. Also, Elon — when you spec the family touchpoint tracker, think about couples as a use case. Shared accountability between spouses could be a premium feature down the line.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Love the couples angle, Dave. That's a content series in itself — "Lever Together" or something. But let's not get ahead of ourselves. Blog post first, feature spec first, then we layer.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, landing this. Top priorities: one, Joshua needs to define the Faith and Family content vision and voice — that's foundational and nothing moves without it. Two, we need a feature spec for the Faith and Family module so engineering can start building. Three, Alex gets the blog post drafted to start generating interest. On approved action items, here's what we're tracking: [AGENT] Draft next blog post, angled on Faith and Family as Lever's differentiator — that's Alex. [JOSHUA] Review 17 BUY signals — Dave, that's still outstanding and high priority even though it's wealth-side. [JOSHUA] Review current positions and position sizing — Dave, keep those in front of Joshua. [AGENT] Monitor Fear and Greed index at 34 — Dave, keep that running in the background. For Faith and Family specifically, we need Joshua in the loop before next standup. That's the unlock. We're done.

actionItems.text: Review current positions: AEE, EWA, EFA, XLV, XLE, VNQ, TLT, GLD, AKAM
Review position sizing and risk parameters for 9 active position(s)

actionItems.agentOutput: F&G: 37.3 (Fear). Sideways regime. SPY/QQQ both sideways. No immediate action needed.

topPriorities: Joshua defines Faith and Family content vision and theological voice for the Lever app
Feature spec for Faith and Family module — devotional engine, family touchpoint tracker, Lever score integration
Blog post drafted positioning Faith and Family as Lever's key differentiator in the market
